rulings.Case Title: MURPHY PROPERTIES INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. USA WASTE OF CALIFORNIA, INC., A DELAWARE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Strike And/Or Tax Memo Of Costs Claimed By Deft Usa Waste Of Californiam Inc
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 21, 2014, Line 1: PLAINTIFF MURPHY PROPERTIES INC.'S Motion To Strike And/Or Tax Memo Of Costs Claimed By Defendant USA Waste Of California, Inc. Plaintiff's motion to tax is granted in its entirety and defendant's motion for attorney's fees is denied without prejudice pursuant to CC 1717. It is premature to determine the prevailing party because this case is still pending. Defendant's cases are distinguishable because they involve petitions to compel arbitration. Plaintiff's motion to enforce settlement and motion for reconsideration are not analogous to those cases. Under the instant circumstances, the motion to enforce settlement is not the type of discreet legal proceeding where fees should be awarded. The case is still open and generally there is only one prevailing party in an action. In Frog Creek Partners, LLC v. Vance Brown, Inc. (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 515, 543, the court notes that cases outside the arbitration context support the conclusion that there can only be one prevailing party. The language of section 12 of the parties' Settlement Agreement does not supersede CC 1717. See Presley of So. Cal. v. Whelan (1983) 146 Cal.App.3d 959 (no interim award of fees allowed even though the contract stated that the prevailing party in any proceeding relating to the contract shall be entitled to recover attorneys fees). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL BOSCHETTO VS. VOSS BORETA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 21, 2014, Line 2: CROSS DEFENDANTS RON BORETA, JOHN BORETA, CRAIG BORETA, DAVID FREAR, FLAMINGO PARADISE PARTNERS, AND ARACELI LUGO, AS THE SPECIAL ADMINISTRATOR OF THE ESTATE OF VASO BORETA AND AS SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE OF THE VASO BORETA 2000 TRUST'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Overruled. The Second Amended Cross-Complaint ("SACC") adequately alleges facts that Cross-Plaintiffs performed their obligations under the oral agreements. "Such conduct satisfies the evidentiary function of the statute of frauds by confirming that a bargain was in fact reached" and as a result, the statute of frauds is not a bar to the breach of contract causes of action. (In re Marriage of Benson (2005) 36 Cal.4th 1096, 1108-1109.) The SACC is not so uncertain that the Cross-Defendants cannot reasonably respond. The request to strike the term "Boreta" from the SACC is denied. It is presumptively within Cross-Defendants' knowledge which Cross-Defendants entered into agreements with Cross-Complainants. Further, any alleged ambiguities arising from the use of the term "Boreta" can be clarified in discovery and/or at trial. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: CHARLOTTE MIMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Claimant To Sign Medical Authorizations
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, July 21, 2014, Line 1, RESPONDENT METLIFE's Motion To Compel Claimant To Sign Medical Authorizations Pro Tem Judge Douglas Robbins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The tentative ruling exceeds the system character limits. The Pro Tem Judge will email the parties and/or their counsel the tentative ruling prior to 3 pm. If any party does not receive the tentative ruling by 3 pm, please email the Pro Tem Judge at drobbins@woodrobbins.com and cc: all other parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to drobbins@woodrobbins.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GINELLE CLOAR VS. KELLY MAZZUCCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 21, 2014, Line 5: DEFENDANTS KELLY MAZZUCCO and THOMAS MAZZUCCO'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' Motion to Strike is granted with leave to amend to permit Plaintiff to plead additional facts establishing that Defendants' conduct amounted to a conscious disregard of the safety of others and that Defendants' conduct was an extreme departure from the ordinary standard of conduct or connoted such a lack of care to impart a presumption of a passive and indifferent attitude toward the outcome. (Santa Barbara v. Sup. Ct. (2007) 41 Cal.4th 747, 754; Calvillo-Silva v. Home Grocery (1998) 19 Cal.4th 714, 729.) rulings.Case Title: DOREEN SINGH VS. EAST WEST BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 21, 2014, Line 9: PLAINTIFF DOREEN SINGH'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. The court sustains without leave to amend as to defense 1 [failure to state a cause of action]. That is not an affirmative defense because an affirmative defense sets forth facts that diminish a cause of action or show that it is not cognizable even if the allegations in the complaint are true. See FPI Development, Inc. v. Al Nahashima (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 367, 383; Goddard v. Fulton (1863) 21 Cal.430, 436. A failure to state a cause action is grounds for a demurrer or motion for judgment on the pleadings. The court sustains without leave to amend as to defenses 20 [no basis for class claim], 21 [Plaintiff lacks standing as class representative], 22 [facts do not warrant class certification], and 24 [improper UCL representative action] because those are reasons to oppose a motion for class certification. They are not defenses. Defense 32 [reservation of rights to raise new defenses] is not cognizable and the court sustains the demurrer to that defense without leave to amend. The court overrules as to defense 2 [statute of limitations]. The defense complies with CCP 458 [pleading statute of limitations defense] because defendant highlights the specific statute that allegedly time bars the claims. The court sustains with 20 days leave to amend as to the remaining defenses. They do not meet the pleading requirements of FPI Development. They are totally conclusory and defendant needs to allege ultimate facts to support the defenses. Lastly, there is nothing improper about defendant's request for attorney's fees and a subsequent motion regarding fees will resolve that issue. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
